journal_entry.rationale: Recording utility expense incurred but not yet paid. Debit Utilities Expense (expense increase, slot c=1) to recognize the cost of utilities consumed during the period. Credit Accounts Payable (liability increase, slot a=1) to record the obligation to pay the utility provider. This follows the accrual basis of accounting under IFRS, recognizing expenses when incurred rather than when cash is paid.

output.reason: Accruing a utility bill recognizes the expense incurred. The debit side records utilities expense increase (expense increase). The corresponding credit would be to accounts payable/accrued liabilities.

debit_classifier.reason: Accruing a utility bill recognizes the expense incurred. The debit side records utilities expense increase (expense increase). The corresponding credit would be to accounts payable/accrued liabilities.

output.reason: Accruing a utility bill means recognizing the expense now even though it hasn't been paid yet. The credit side records the liability increase (utilities payable) for the amount owed. This creates an accounts payable or accrued liability = liability increase.

credit_classifier.reason: Accruing a utility bill means recognizing the expense now even though it hasn't been paid yet. The credit side records the liability increase (utilities payable) for the amount owed. This creates an accounts payable or accrued liability = liability increase.
